shared_properties: Spike hazards keep a 16x16 px footprint per tile and a rust-red tip silhouette with one-pixel outlines.
Collision read stays immediate from a top-down glance during fast descent because every spike tip points into open travel space.
differences: This sheet supplies four static spike variants for floor, left-wall, right-wall, and dense-cluster placements.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Hazard size, damage rule, and placement density grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Oxidized metal tone and dungeon hazard contrast grounding.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use TextureAtlasLayout for four hazard variants while collision stays on separate rectangular or polygon hitboxes in gameplay code.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/spike_cluster_tiles.png through AssetServer and address variant frames by tile placement metadata.
runtime_notes: No animation timer is required because spikes are static hazards.
Leave one transparent pixel around outward points to avoid clipping when packed in the atlas.

context.role: Primary static damage hazard used in the tutorial band and every later dungeon section.
context.usage: Placed on floors and walls to punish incorrect friction timing without horizontal movement controls.

deliverables.notes: Four 16x16 spike variants for floor, wall-left, wall-right, and dense-cluster placements.

acceptance_criteria: Sheet size is exactly 64x16 px with four 16x16 variants.
Every variant reads as hazardous under grayscale preview because the spike tips stay brighter than the base plate.
Transparent padding prevents neighboring spike variants from bleeding during atlas sampling.
